question:
A question is followed by two statements numbered (I) and (II). You have to decide whether the data provided in the statements are sufficient to answer the question. Read both the statements carefully and decide the appropriate answer.

Six people P, Q, R, S, T and U are sitting in a straight line facing north. Who sits immediately to the left of R?

I. Q sits second from one of the extreme ends. Only one person sits between Q and R. S sits to the immediate right of R.

II. U sits at one of the extreme ends. T sits third to the right of U. P sits to the immediate left of R.
option_1: Data in statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in statement II is not
option_2: Data in both statements I and II together is NOT sufficient
option_3: Data in both statements I and II together is sufficient
option_4: Data in statement II alone is sufficient while data in statement I is not
solution_text:
Statement I:

  • S sits immediate right of R → R S
  • Q and R have one person between
  • Q is second from an end

👉 Different arrangements possible
👉 Left of R is not fixed

⇒ Statement I is NOT sufficient

Statement II:

  • P sits immediate left of R

⇒ Statement II is sufficient

Final Answer:
Data in statement II alone is sufficient while data in statement I is not

question:
A decision maker must choose between two operational plans:

Plan X: Guarantees a performance level that meets 90% of the target.
Plan Y: Offers a 60% chance of exceeding the target by 20% and a 40% chance of falling short by 10%.

Question: Assuming the decision maker is risk-neutral and based solely on the expected outcome, which plan should be preferred?
option_1: Plan X
option_2: Plan Y
option_3: Both plans are equivalent
option_4: Further details on the target are required
solution_text:
Explanation:

  • In a risk-neutral situation, the decision is based on expected value (average outcome), not certainty.
  • Plan X:
    It guarantees 90% of the target, so its expected value = 90%
  • Plan Y:
    • 60% chance of achieving 120% of target
    • 40% chance of achieving 90% of target
      Expected value = (0.6 × 120) + (0.4 × 90)
      = 72 + 36 = 108%
  • Since 108% (Plan Y) > 90% (Plan X), Plan Y gives a higher average outcome.

👉 Therefore, a risk-neutral decision maker will prefer Plan Y.

question:
Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option.

Statement: Increasing screen time among students leads to reduced physical activity, eye strain, and decreased academic performance, thereby affecting overall development.

Assumptions:
I. Excessive screen time can reduce physical activity levels.
II. Academic performance is not influenced by lifestyle habits.
III. Prolonged screen exposure can cause health-related issues like eye strain.
option_1: Only I and III are implicit
option_2: Only II and III are implicit
option_3: Only I is implicit
option_4: All assumptions are implicit
solution_text:
Explanation:

  • The statement says that increasing screen time causes reduced physical activity, eye strain, and lower academic performance.
  • Assumption I is valid because reduced physical activity is directly linked to excessive screen time.
  • Assumption III is also valid as eye strain is a known effect of prolonged screen exposure.
  • Assumption II is incorrect because the statement itself suggests that lifestyle habits (like screen time) do affect academic performance.

👉 Therefore, only I and III are implicit.

question: Select the next term: Perception, Comprehension, Judgement, ______
option_1: Criticism
option_2: Evaluation
option_3: Decision
option_4: Intuition
solution_text:
Explanation:

This question is based on a logical progression of mental or cognitive processes.

Let us understand each term in sequence:

  • Perception means noticing or becoming aware of something. It is the first stage, where the mind receives information.
  • Comprehension means understanding that information. After perceiving something, we try to grasp its meaning.
  • Judgement means forming an opinion about what has been understood. At this stage, the mind starts analysing and comparing.
  • The next natural step is Evaluation, which means assessing the value, quality, or importance of something in a more complete way.

So, the order becomes:

Perception → Comprehension → Judgement → Evaluation

question: Which of the following is NOT a member of the Selection Committee for the Director of CBI?
option_1: Prime Minister
option_2: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha
option_3: Chief Justice of India
option_4: Home Minister
solution_text:
The Home Minister is NOT a member of the Selection Committee for appointing the Director of the CBI. The committee consists of the Prime Minister (Chairperson), the Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha, and the Chief Justice of India (or a Supreme Court judge nominated by him).

Detailed Explanation

Composition of the Selection Committee

  • Prime Minister → Serves as the Chairperson of the committee.
  • Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha → Ensures bipartisan representation.
  • Chief Justice of India (or a Supreme Court judge nominated by him) → Provides judicial oversight.

This three-member committee was established under the Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, amending the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946.

Why the Home Minister is not included

  • The Home Minister is not part of the committee to maintain independence and reduce executive dominance.
  • The CBI functions under the Department of Personnel and Training (DoPT), which is under the Prime Minister’s Office, not directly under the Ministry of Home Affairs.

Correct Answer Recap

  • Prime Minister ✅ Member
  • Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha ✅ Member
  • Chief Justice of India ✅ Member
  • Home Minister ❌ Not a member

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the gases used in fire extinguishers:

I: Carbon Dioxide (CO2) is widely used in fire extinguishers for suppression.

II: Carbon Monoxide, Nitrogen Oxide, and Sulphur Dioxide are unsuitable for fire suppression due to their toxic nature or ineffectiveness.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
option_1: Only I
option_2: Only II
option_3: Both I and II
option_4: Neither I nor II
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: (C) Both I and II

Explanation

  • Carbon Dioxide (CO₂) is the gas commonly used in fire extinguishers.
  • It works by:
    • Displacing oxygen around the fire (removing the element needed for combustion).
    • Cooling the flames.
  • CO₂ extinguishers are especially effective for electrical fires and flammable liquid fires because they do not leave residue.

Options Recap

  • Carbon Monoxide ❌ → Poisonous gas, not used in extinguishers.
  • Nitrogen Oxide ❌ → Not used for fire suppression.
  • Carbon Dioxide ✅ → Correct; widely used in fire extinguishers.
  • Sulphur Dioxide ❌ → Toxic gas, not used in extinguishers.

question:
Consider the following statements about the Ramosi Uprising (1822):

Statement I: The uprising was primarily triggered by economic distress caused by high land revenue and severe famine in Western Maharashtra.

Statement II: The British government suppressed the revolt through strictly military means and refused to adopt any conciliatory measures toward the Ramosis.

Which of the statements given above is/are incorrect?
option_1: Only I
option_2: Both I and II
option_3: Only II
option_4: Neither I nor II
solution_text:
The Correct Answer is: Only II

Statement II is incorrect because the British did adopt conciliatory policies (such as land grants and Hill Police recruitment) to pacify the community after the initial suppression.

The Ramosi Uprising of 1822 was primarily a reaction against British annexation policies and the resulting economic hardships, especially high land revenue demands and famine conditions in Western Maharashtra.

Background of the Ramosi Uprising (1822)

  • Region: Western Maharashtra, particularly Satara.
  • Community involved: The Ramosis, a tribal group traditionally employed as watchmen and soldiers under the Maratha rulers.
  • Leaders: Initially led by Chittur Singh in 1822, later carried forward by Umaji Naik until 1829.

Causes of the Uprising

  • British Annexation Policies: After the fall of the Maratha Empire, the Ramosis lost their traditional livelihood and privileges.
  • High Land Revenue: The British imposed heavy land taxes, worsening the economic condition of peasants and tribal communities.
  • Famine Conditions: Crop failures and lack of relief measures aggravated discontent.
  • Loss of Employment: Ramosis, who had served as armed guards under Maratha rulers, were left unemployed under British rule.

Nature of the Revolt

  • The Ramosis engaged in guerrilla-style attacks against British establishments.
  • They targeted revenue officials and disrupted British administration in Satara and surrounding areas.
  • The rebellion was marked by violent resistance rather than peaceful protest.

Outcome

  • The British suppressed the uprising but later adopted conciliatory policies to pacify the Ramosis.
  • The revolt inspired later resistance movements in Maharashtra, including those led by Vasudev Balwant Phadke in the late 19th century.

Options Recap

  • Interference in tribal customs ❌ → Not the main cause.
  • Ban on religious ceremonies ❌ → Not relevant here.
  • High land revenue and famine ✅ → Correct cause; economic exploitation triggered the uprising.
  • Forced indigo cultivation ❌ → This was a cause of revolts in Bengal, not Maharashtra.

question: The 'Phillips Curve' shows the relationship between:
option_1: Inflation and Unemployment
option_2: Demand and Supply
option_3: Income and Consumption
option_4: Imports and Exports
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Inflation and Unemployment

Explanation

  • The Phillips Curve is an economic concept that shows the inverse relationship between inflation and unemployment.
  • Proposed by A.W. Phillips (1958), it suggested that:
    • When unemployment is low → inflation tends to be high.
    • When unemployment is high → inflation tends to be low.
  • This trade-off was considered valid in the short run, but in the long run, economists like Milton Friedman and Edmund Phelps argued that the relationship breaks down due to expectations of inflation.

Options Recap

  • Inflation and Unemployment ✅ → Correct; Phillips Curve shows this relationship.
  • Demand and Supply ❌ → Basic market forces, not the Phillips Curve.
  • Income and Consumption ❌ → Related to Keynesian consumption function, not Phillips Curve.
  • Imports and Exports ❌ → Related to trade balance, not Phillips Curve.

question: Which body is responsible for the 'Audit of Local Bodies' in most Indian states?
option_1: Comptroller and Auditor General
option_2: State Finance Commission
option_3: Director of Local Fund Audit
option_4: Ministry of Panchayati Raj
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Director of Local Fund Audit

Explanation

  • In most Indian states, the audit of local bodies (Panchayats, Municipalities, etc.) is conducted by the Director of Local Fund Audit (DLFA).
  • The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) has the authority to audit local bodies, but usually only in a supplementary or supervisory role, not as the primary auditor.
  • The State Finance Commission deals with financial distribution between the state and local bodies, not auditing.
  • The Ministry of Panchayati Raj is responsible for policy and implementation at the central level, not auditing.

Options Recap

  • Comptroller and Auditor General ❌ → Supervisory role, not the primary auditor.
  • State Finance Commission ❌ → Recommends financial devolution, not auditing.
  • Director of Local Fund Audit ✅ → Correct; primary auditor of local bodies in most states.
  • Ministry of Panchayati Raj ❌ → Policy-making, not auditing.

question: Who is known as the 'Father of Carnatic Music'?
option_1: Purandara Dasa
option_2: Tyagaraja
option_3: Muthuswami Dikshitar
option_4: Syama Sastri
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Purandara Dasa

Explanation

  • Purandara Dasa (1484–1564) is regarded as the Father of Carnatic Music.
  • He systematized the teaching of Carnatic music by:
    • Introducing graded lessons (sarali varisai, jantai varisai, alankaras).
    • Composing simple devotional songs for beginners.
    • Standardizing the raga Mayamalavagowla as the basic scale for beginners.
  • His contributions laid the foundation for the structured pedagogy of Carnatic music.

Options Recap

  • Purandara Dasa ✅ → Correct. Known as the Father of Carnatic Music.
  • Tyagaraja ❌ → One of the Trinity of Carnatic Music, but not the founder.
  • Muthuswami Dikshitar ❌ → Another member of the Trinity, famous for kritis.
  • Syama Sastri ❌ → Also part of the Trinity, known for his compositions, but not the father.

question: 'Thermal Inversion' is a phenomenon where:
option_1: Temperature remains constant with altitude.
option_2: Temperature fluctuates with wind speed.
option_3: Temperature decreases with altitude.
option_4: Temperature increases with altitude.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Temperature increases with altitude

Explanation

  • Normally, in the troposphere, temperature decreases with altitude (about 6.5°C per km).
  • But during thermal inversion, this pattern is reversed:
    • Temperature increases with altitude instead of decreasing.
    • A layer of warm air traps cooler air below it.
  • This phenomenon prevents vertical mixing of air, leading to air pollution accumulation near the ground (smog, haze).

Options Recap

  • Temperature remains constant with altitude ❌ → Not correct; that’s an isothermal condition.
  • Temperature fluctuates with wind speed ❌ → Wind speed is not the defining factor.
  • Temperature decreases with altitude ❌ → Normal lapse rate, not inversion.
  • Temperature increases with altitude ✅ → Correct; this is thermal inversion.

question: The 'Sandalwood Mountains' (Malayagiri) were historically associated with:
option_1: Chola Dynasty Kings
option_2: Western Ganga Dynasty
option_3: Kadamba Dynasty Kings
option_4: Hoysala Dynasty Kings
solution_text:
The 'Sandalwood Mountains' (Malayagiri) were historically associated with the Western Ganga Dynasty.

Explanation

  • The Western Ganga Dynasty (350–1000 CE) ruled parts of present-day Karnataka.
  • They were known for their patronage of Jainism and for controlling regions rich in sandalwood forests, including the Malayagiri (Sandalwood Mountains).
  • The dynasty’s association with sandalwood trade and forested regions gave these mountains their historical significance.

Options Recap

  • Chola Dynasty Kings ❌ → Primarily associated with Tamil Nadu and the Coromandel coast, not sandalwood mountains.
  • Western Ganga Dynasty ✅ → Correct; historically linked with sandalwood-rich regions and Malayagiri.
  • Kadamba Dynasty Kings ❌ → Early rulers of Karnataka, but not specifically tied to sandalwood mountains.
  • Hoysala Dynasty Kings ❌ → Flourished later (11th–14th century), known for temple architecture, not sandalwood.

question: 'Barchans' are landforms created by the:
option_1: Rivers
option_2: Glaciers
option_3: Winds
option_4: Waves
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Winds

Explanation

  • Barchans are crescent-shaped sand dunes formed by the action of wind in desert regions.
  • They develop when:
    • Sand supply is limited.
    • Wind blows consistently from one direction.
    • The surface is hard enough to prevent sand from being absorbed.
  • The horns of a barchan point downwind, while the steep slope (slip face) is on the leeward side.

Options Recap

  • Rivers ❌ → Create landforms like deltas, floodplains, oxbow lakes.
  • Glaciers ❌ → Form moraines, drumlins, eskers.
  • Winds ✅ → Correct; responsible for barchans in deserts.
  • Waves ❌ → Form coastal landforms like beaches, spits, lagoons.

question: Which classical dance is characterized by the use of 'Mudras' and heavy facial expressions called 'Navarasas'?

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the temple architecture of the Chalukya Dynasty at Pattadakal:

I: The Pattadakal temple complex, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is renowned for uniquely synthesizing both Dravidian and Nagara architectural styles.

II: The Virupaksha Temple, the most prominent structure at the site, was commissioned by Queen Lokamahadevi to commemorate the Chalukya victory over the Pallavas.

III: The Chalukyas were the sole patrons of the site, with all temples at Pattadakal being strictly dedicated exclusively to Jain traditions, excluding any Hindu deities.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
option_1: Only I
option_2: Only I and II
option_3: Only II and III
option_4: All of the these
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Only I and II

Statement III is incorrect: The site includes shrines dedicated to various Hindu traditions (primarily Shiva and Vishnu) alongside Jain traditions, making the claim of "exclusive" dedication to Jainism false.

Explanation:

  • The Pattadakal temples (UNESCO World Heritage Site, Karnataka) were built by the Chalukyas of Badami during the 7th–8th centuries CE.
  • Pattadakal is unique because it showcases a blend of Dravidian (South Indian) and Nagara (North Indian) architectural styles.
  • Famous temples at Pattadakal include:
    • Virupaksha Temple (built by Queen Lokamahadevi to commemorate Chalukya victory over the Pallavas).
    • Mallikarjuna Temple.
    • Other shrines dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu, and Jain traditions.
  • The site reflects the Chalukyas’ patronage of art, architecture, and religion.

Options Recap

  • Pallava Dynasty ❌ → Known for temples at Mahabalipuram and Kanchipuram, not Pattadakal.
  • Chalukya Dynasty ✅ → Correct; builders of Pattadakal temples.
  • Chola Dynasty ❌ → Famous for Brihadeeswarar Temple (Thanjavur), not Pattadakal.
  • Rashtrakuta Dynasty ❌ → Known for Kailasa Temple at Ellora, not Pattadakal.

question: 'Kettle Holes' are landforms produced by the:
option_1: Glacier
option_2: River
option_3: Wind
option_4: Wave
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Glacier

Explanation

  • Kettle Holes are depressions or pits formed in glacial landscapes.
  • They originate when:
    • Large blocks of ice get buried in glacial till or outwash.
    • Later, as the ice melts, the overlying sediments collapse, leaving a depression.
  • These depressions often fill with water, forming kettle lakes.
  • Commonly found in regions once covered by continental glaciers (e.g., North America, Northern Europe).

Options Recap

  • Glacier ✅ → Correct; kettle holes are glacial landforms.
  • River ❌ → Rivers form oxbow lakes, floodplains, deltas.
  • Wind ❌ → Wind forms dunes, loess deposits, deflation hollows.
  • Wave ❌ → Waves form coastal features like spits, lagoons, cliffs.

question: Which part of the brain is primarily responsible for maintaining posture and balance?
option_1: Cerebrum
option_2: Cerebellum
option_3: Medulla Oblongata
option_4: Hypothalamus
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Cerebellum

Explanation

  • It receives input from the sensory systems, spinal cord, and other parts of the brain, then integrates this information to fine‑tune motor activity.

Options Recap

  • Cerebrum ❌ → Controls higher functions like thinking, memory, emotions, and voluntary actions.
  • Cerebellum ✅ → Maintains posture, balance, and smooth coordination of movements.
  • Medulla Oblongata ❌ → Regulates involuntary functions like breathing, heartbeat, and digestion.
  • Hypothalamus ❌ → Controls homeostasis, hunger, thirst, temperature regulation, and endocrine functions.

question: Which statement accurately describes the 'Veto Power' of the Indian President?
option_1: The President can exercise 'Pocket Veto' for Constitutional Amendment Bills.
option_2: The President has 'Qualified Veto' like the American President.
option_3: 'Suspensive Veto' cannot be used for Money Bills.
option_4: The President must give assent to all State Bills reserved by the Governor.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: 'Suspensive Veto' cannot be used for Money Bills.

Explanation of Veto Powers of the Indian President

The Indian President enjoys three types of veto powers:

  1. Absolute Veto
    • The President can reject a bill entirely.
    • Used rarely, usually when a Private Member’s Bill is passed.
  2. Suspensive Veto
    • The President can return a bill (except a Money Bill) for reconsideration.
    • If Parliament passes it again, the President must give assent.
    • ❌ Cannot be used for Money Bills (Article 110).
  3. Pocket Veto
    • The President can withhold assent indefinitely by not acting on the bill.
    • Example: President Zail Singh used it in 1986 against the Indian Post Office (Amendment) Bill.

Options Analysis

  • The President can exercise 'Pocket Veto' for Constitutional Amendment Bills. ❌ → Wrong. For Constitutional Amendment Bills, the President must give assent (Article 368). No veto applies.
  • The President has 'Qualified Veto' like the American President. ❌ → Wrong. India does not have a qualified veto system.
  • 'Suspensive Veto' cannot be used for Money Bills. ✅ → Correct. This is the accurate statement.
  • The President must give assent to all State Bills reserved by the Governor. ❌ → Wrong. The President may assent, withhold, or return (except Money Bills).

question: Which soil type covers the largest area in India?
option_1: Black Soil
option_2: Red Soil
option_3: Alluvial Soil
option_4: Laterite Soil
solution_text:
Alluvial soil covers the largest area in India, accounting for about 43% of the country’s total land area. It is mainly found in the Northern Plains and river basins, making it the most extensive and agriculturally important soil type.

Key Facts about Alluvial Soil

  • Coverage: ~43% of India’s land area
  • Formation: Deposited by rivers such as the Ganga, Yamuna, Brahmaputra, Indus, and their tributaries.
  • Regions: Northern Plains (Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, West Bengal, Assam), parts of Gujarat and Rajasthan, and coastal deltas.
  • Fertility: Highly fertile, rich in potash and lime but generally poor in nitrogen and humus.
  • Crops Supported: Wheat, rice, sugarcane, cotton, pulses, and oilseeds.

question: 'Viscosity' of a liquid generally decreases with:

option_2: Increase in temperature.
option_3: Decrease in density.
option_4: Increase in volume.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Increase in temperature

Explanation

  • Viscosity = resistance of a liquid to flow.
  • For liquids, viscosity decreases with increase in temperature because:
    • Higher temperature → molecules gain kinetic energy.
    • Intermolecular forces weaken.
    • Liquid flows more easily.

Options Recap

  • Increase in pressure ❌ → Pressure has negligible effect on liquid viscosity.
  • Increase in temperature ✅ → Correct; viscosity decreases.
  • Decrease in density ❌ → Not a direct relation.
  • Increase in volume ❌ → No direct effect on viscosity.

question: How many numbers are there from 100 to 600 which are neither divisible by 4 nor by 6?

Total Divisible = n₄ + n₆ − n₁₂
= 126 + 84 − 42 = 168

Numbers NEITHER divisible by 4 nor 6:
= 501 − 168 = 333

question:
Read the following paragraph carefully and answer the given questions:

Lithium-ion batteries have emerged as the cornerstone of the modern portable electronics revolution, powering everything from smartphones to electric vehicles (EVs). These batteries operate on the principle of lithium ions moving between the anode and cathode through an electrolyte. During discharge, ions move from the negative anode to the positive cathode, creating an electron flow that powers the device. The high energy density and low self-discharge rates make them superior to traditional nickel-cadmium alternatives. However, the surge in demand has triggered significant geopolitical and environmental concerns regarding the extraction of raw materials like lithium, cobalt, and nickel, often dubbed as "urban mining" challenges.

Despite their dominance, lithium-ion technology faces inherent limitations, particularly regarding safety and lifespan. Issues such as thermal runaway—a condition where an increase in temperature leads to a further increase in temperature, potentially causing fires—remain a primary concern for manufacturers. Researchers are currently exploring solid-state electrolytes as a safer alternative to the flammable liquid electrolytes used today. Furthermore, the push for a circular economy has intensified efforts in battery recycling. Efficiently recovering high-purity metals from spent cells is not just an environmental necessity but a strategic imperative to reduce dependency on volatile global supply chains.

Q- According to the passage, what is the primary mechanism that powers a device using a lithium-ion battery?
option_1: The movement of electrons from the electrolyte to the negative anode during the charging phase.
option_2: The flow of lithium ions from the negative anode to the positive cathode during the discharge.
option_3: The chemical reaction between nickel and cadmium within the battery's solid-state electrolyte.
option_4: The thermal runaway process that generates heat and converts it into electrical energy flow.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: The flow of lithium ions from the negative anode to the positive cathode during the discharge.

Explanation:

  • Lithium-ion batteries work on the principle of lithium ions moving between the anode and cathode through the electrolyte.
  • During discharge, lithium ions travel from the negative anode to the positive cathode, while electrons flow through the external circuit, powering the device.
  • This movement of ions and electrons is the primary mechanism that generates usable electrical energy.

Q- What does the term "thermal runaway" imply in the context of the passage?
option_1: A process where the battery runs out of charge faster than the manufacturer's initial estimates.
option_2: A safety feature that shuts down the battery when it reaches a specific temperature threshold.
option_3: A dangerous cycle where rising temperatures trigger further heating, leading to potential fires.
option_4: The efficient movement of ions through the electrolyte to maximize the battery's energy output.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: A dangerous cycle where rising temperatures trigger further heating, leading to potential fires.

Explanation:

  • In lithium-ion batteries, thermal runaway is a hazardous feedback loop.
  • When the battery temperature rises, it accelerates internal chemical reactions.
  • These reactions release more heat, which further increases the temperature.
  • This self-reinforcing cycle can lead to overheating, fire, or even explosions, making it one of the most critical safety concerns in battery technology.

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

She said to me, "I will be waiting for you at the cafe at 5 PM tomorrow, so please don't be late."
option_1: She informed me that she would wait for me at the cafe at 5 PM the next day and told me not to be late.
option_2: She said to me that she would be waiting for me at the cafe at 5 PM tomorrow and asked me not to be late.
option_3: She told me that she will be waiting for me at the cafe at 5 PM the next day and requested me not to be late.
option_4: She told me that she would be waiting for me at the cafe at 5 PM the next day and requested me not to be late.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: She told me that she would be waiting for me at the cafe at 5 PM the next day and requested me not to be late.

Explanation:

  • The original sentence is in direct speech: She said to me, "I will be waiting for you at the cafe at 5 PM tomorrow, so please don't be late."
  • When converting to indirect speech:
    • "said to me" → "told me"
    • "I will be waiting" → "she would be waiting" (future tense changes to conditional in reported speech).
    • "tomorrow" → "the next day" (time reference changes).
    • "please don't be late" → "requested me not to be late" (request form).

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Option A (would wait) → Changes continuous aspect incorrectly; original is will be waiting.
  • Option B (tomorrow) → Time reference not changed; should be the next day.
  • Option C (will be waiting) → Incorrect tense; should be would be waiting.

question:

Explanation:

  • In passive voice, the object (the exotic plants) becomes the subject: “The exotic plants… were being watered…”

  • Option A (were watered) → Simple past, not past continuous.
  • Option B (are being watered) → Present continuous, incorrect tense.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Option A (was being refused) → Incorrect tense; suggests continuous action, not simple past.
  • Option B (had been refused) → Past perfect tense, not matching the original.
  • Option D (is refused) → Present tense, not matching the past tense of the original.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given IDIOM:

To jump through hoops
option_1: To engage in a series of difficult tasks to please someone.
option_2: To participate in a sporting event with great enthusiasm.
option_3: To avoid taking responsibility for a major professional error.
option_4: To find a shortcut while solving a complex mathematical problem.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: To engage in a series of difficult tasks to please someone. (किसी को खुश करने के लिए कठिन कार्यों की श्रृंखला में शामिल होना)

Explanation

  • Idiom: “To jump through hoops”
  • Meaning: To go through many obstacles, challenges, or difficult tasks, usually to satisfy someone or achieve something (किसी को खुश करने या कुछ हासिल करने के लिए कठिनाइयों से गुजरना) ।
  • Example: She had to jump through hoops to get her visa approved. (उसे अपना वीज़ा मंज़ूर करवाने के लिए कई कठिन प्रक्रियाओं से गुजरना पड़ा) ।

question:
One Word Substitution:

A person who lives at the same time as another.
option_1: Colleague
option_2: Contemporary
option_3: Compatriot
option_4: Comrade
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Contemporary (समकालीन/एक ही समय में रहने वाला व्यक्ति)

Explanation

  • Word meaning: A person who lives at the same time as another → Contemporary (समकालीन)।
  • Example: Kalidasa was a contemporary of Chandragupta II. (कालिदास चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय के समकालीन थे) ।

Options Recap

  • Colleague (सहकर्मी) ❌ → Someone you work with, not necessarily same time.
  • Contemporary (समकालीन) ✅ → Correct one-word substitution.
  • Compatriot (हमवतन) ❌ → Fellow countryman.
  • Comrade (साथी/मित्र) ❌ → Companion, often in political or military context.

question:
Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word:

The judicial system must remain ______ to ensure that justice is served without any bias or external political pressure.
option_1: Ingenuous
option_2: Impenitent
option_3: Impartial
option_4: Improvident
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Impartial (निष्पक्ष/पक्षपात रहित)

Explanation

  • Sentence: The judicial system must remain ______ to ensure that justice is served without any bias or external political pressure.
  • The most appropriate word is Impartial, meaning fair, unbiased, neutral (निष्पक्ष/पक्षपात रहित) ।
  • Example: A judge must always be impartial in delivering justice. (न्यायाधीश को हमेशा न्याय देने में निष्पक्ष होना चाहिए) ।

Options Recap

  • Ingenuous (भोला/निर्दोष) ❌ → Means innocent or naive, not suitable.
  • Impenitent (पश्चाताप रहित) ❌ → Means not feeling regret, irrelevant.
  • Impartial (निष्पक्ष/पक्षपात रहित) ✅ → Correct choice.
  • Improvident (अविवेकी/भविष्य की चिंता न करने वाला) ❌ → Means careless about future, not suitable.
